key_insight: A Monte Carlo simulation framework can turn published aggregate rare-cancer trial data into patient-level risk predictions, demonstrated in Ewing sarcoma.
clinical_relevance: This could help refine survivorship surveillance, recurrence-risk stratification, and future trial design when individual-level data are unavailable.
method: Integrates genetic risk, serial biomarkers, ctDNA-MRD, and treatment-related mortality
Models long-term toxicity across five organ systems
evidence_signal: In Ewing sarcoma, the framework was validated against data from more than 3,400 patients, with 3.2% mean absolute error across 23 efficacy endpoints and all 20 toxicity endpoints within published confidence intervals.
watch_item: The approach is validated on one rare cancer using published aggregate data, so transfer to other cancers and real-world patient-level use still needs prospective validation.
paper_title: Multi-Timepoint Risk Stratification in Rare Cancers: A Computational Framework Validated against Published Ewing Sarcoma Trial Data
